reviews.review_msg: If you aren't with doing mid in a class don't take her - once she had us come to class and she herself did not even show up and decided that we could take the time to talk to our peers/group while she said she would be available on zoom .... even though no link was provided. No clear outline for grading, did not give marks till the end. Goodluck!

For an upper div seminar class, this was a joke. waste of money. for the month of september we had no syllabus, just in the dark about where the class was going and brenda admitted that she was not prioritizing the class becuase she was busy with her own things.. she started crying. i feel like i did not learn anything i didn't alr kno.

While Brenda's teaching methods are not what we are used to as upper-division students, she is such an amazing and caring woman who really wants you to succeed. Step outside of your boxes in this class because what she does is not traditional, but that is not a bad thing at all. If you're flexible and willing to do the actual work, you'll do great
Interesting course, extremely helpful teacher. She's approachable and a really nice lady

Jordana was one of the nicest profs I have ever had at SFU. She goes out of her way to make sure students are doing well. Assignments are very fair and marked fair too. She tries to make classes interesting. Recommend taking a class with her.

Jordana is the best prof I have ever had at SFU. She is so unbelievably kind and welcoming and really strives to make sure everyone understands each concept. She has a clear love and passion for crim and teaching, which makes lectures much more enjoyable. I will be taking any class she teaches from here on out!
